Politics.headline: House Republicans subpoena prosecutor for records tied to Stephen Miller protester
Politics.detail: The demand comes after Rep. Jim Jordan, chairman of the House Judiciary Committee, opened an inquiry into Parisa Dehghani-Tafti, Arlington County's lead prosecutor.

Politics.headline: Russia offers to halt sharing intel with Iran if US does the same with Ukraine: Report
Politics.detail: Russian President Vladimir Putin has reportedly offered the United States a strategic swap: Russia will halt all intelligence sharing with Iran if the U.S. does the same with Ukraine. The Trump administration rejected the offer, sparking concern among European officials.

Politics.headline: Trump's Iran war devolves into a 'full blown crisis': report
Politics.detail: Experts are calling for de-escalation as President Donald Trump's war against Iran continues to expand in scope, with Israel and Iran trading strikes at key energy infrastructure. War games experts have warned of such outcomes for years, but Trump insists that 'nobody' briefed him of such dire possibilities.

Politics.headline: Proposal to cut electricity bills by £30 per year to be brought to Stormont Executive for approval
Politics.detail: A proposal to cut electricity bills by £30 per year is to be brought to the Executive for approval next week, from an £81 million fund over the next 3 years. Every household is set to get a £30 reduction in their bills for each of the next 3 years.

Politics.headline: Trump cites Pearl Harbor to defend Iran war decision: 'Who knows surprise better than Japan?'
Politics.detail: During a meeting with the Japanese Prime Minister, President Trump referenced Pearl Harbor when discussing the ongoing war with Iran. He stated that Japan was 'stepping up to the plate' to support the US war, but did not offer specific details.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stock markets dip for fourth straight week over US-Israel war on Iran
Finance & Economy.detail: The US stock markets have declined for the fourth consecutive week due to concerns surrounding the US-Israel war on Iran, creating uncertainty in the market.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK borrowing costs hit highest since 2008 as markets expect up to three interest rate rises
Finance & Economy.detail: UK borrowing costs have reached their highest level since 2008, as markets anticipate up to three interest rate increases.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks today: Dow crashes 444 pts, Nasdaq 2%, S&P over 1% as Iran war hurts US stock markets most in 4 weeks.
Finance & Economy.detail: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 444 points, the Nasdaq dropped 2%, and the S&P 500 decreased by over 1% as the Iran war significantly impacted US stock markets, marking the most substantial decline in four weeks.

Finance & Economy.headline: Small businesses reeling from tariffs brace for spiking oil prices.
Finance & Economy.detail: Small businesses, already struggling with tariffs, are now preparing for rising oil prices, further adding to their economic challenges.

Finance & Economy.headline: DiDi imposes fuel surcharge as rising petrol prices squeeze drivers.
Finance & Economy.detail: DiDi is implementing a fuel surcharge to alleviate the financial burden on drivers caused by rising petrol prices, with some drivers paying up to $100 extra per week for fuel.

Technology.headline: MacBook Neo Just Broke an Apple Sales Record, Shipping Delays Continue
Technology.detail: The laptop is a record-breaking release for sales to first-time Mac owners, according to Tim Cook.

Technology.headline: US Disrupts Aisuru, Botnet Behind Record-Breaking 30Tbps DDoS Attacks
Technology.detail: The operation targeted four botnets, estimated to have infected millions of devices globally, including TV boxes, web cameras, and Wi-Fi routers.

Technology.headline: Amazon is developing a new smartphone codenamed "Transformer," led by the same devices unit behind Alexa, with a focus on AI integration and mobile personalization
Technology.detail: Amazon is reportedly working on a new AI-focused smartphone codenamed "Transformer", spearheaded by the same team responsible for Alexa. The device will emphasize AI integration and personalized mobile experiences.

Technology.headline: AI's demand for data could cause tight storage chip supplies, Solidigm executive says
Technology.detail: According to a Solidigm executive, the increasing demand for data by AI systems could lead to a shortage of storage chips. High-bandwidth memory is crucial for servers used by companies like Nvidia, and shortages could potentially last until 2030.

Technology.headline: AI-Powered Cyberattacks Raise Alarm Among IT Leaders
Technology.detail: Nearly eight out of 10 global IT decision-makers say artificial intelligence poses a significant security threat as AI-driven attacks accelerate in speed and scale. AI is becoming a force multiplier for cybercriminals, helping threat actors automate phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance while accelerating th.

Science.headline: Shingles Vaccine Might Double as Powerful Heart Protector
Science.detail: In people already at high risk, a shingles vaccine cut major cardiac events by 46% and deaths by an impressive 66% within a year, leading scientists to believe that preventing shingles may also stop clot-related issues.

Science.headline: Mosquitoes get the 'I'm full' signal from their butts, not their brains
Science.detail: New research reveals that mosquitoes receive the signal to stop feeding from stretch receptors in their abdomen, rather than from their brains, which could provide new avenues for controlling mosquito populations.

Science.headline: An immune signaling pathway drives pain in arthritis, researchers discover
Science.detail: A recent study has identified an immune signaling pathway that contributes to pain in arthritis, offering a potential new target for pain management in this condition.

Science.headline: Hubble Captures Breakup of Comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS)
Science.detail: The Hubble Space Telescope has captured images of the breakup of Comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS), providing valuable insights into the composition and behavior of comets as they approach the sun.

Science.headline: Amid vaccine policy whiplash, here's how a pediatrician talks to families
Science.detail: With shifting vaccine policies, a pediatrician shares strategies for discussing vaccinations with families, emphasizing clear communication and addressing concerns.

Health.headline: UNICEF: 99% of foods marketed to Filipino children fail global health standards
Health.detail: UNICEF reports that 99% of food products marketed to children in the Philippines do not meet WHO standards, contributing to rising obesity rates and prompting calls for stronger legislation. The agency warns that if this trend continues, about half the global population could be overweight or obese by 2035.

Health.headline: NTU Singapore, UMC Utrecht Unite on Global Health Issues
Health.detail: NTU Singapore and UMC Utrecht are collaborating on healthcare, medical research, and education, focusing on areas like AI in healthcare, regenerative medicine, and public health. The partnership aims to foster research, innovation, and academic exchange between the two institutions.

Health.headline: Match Day 2026: Is Family Medicine Half-Full or Half-Empty?
Health.detail: Medscape Medical News reports on Match Day 2026, noting that medical students are gravitating towards higher-paying fields or those with better work-life balance when choosing their residencies. This trend raises questions about the future of family medicine.

Health.headline: High Blood Pressure, High Blood Sugar and High Cholesterol May Harm the Kidneys, Health Promotion Administration Urges Regular Checkups to Protect Kidney Health
Health.detail: The Health Promotion Administration in Taiwan urges regular checkups to protect kidney health, as high blood pressure, high blood sugar, and high cholesterol are major risk factors for chronic kidney disease. Statistics show that about one in five people over 40 with these conditions has abnormal kidney function.

Health.headline: Dr. Brent Roeder Talks Colorectal Health at Next Walk with a Doc
Health.detail: Stormont Vail Health is hosting a Walk With A Doc event on March 21, 2026, featuring Dr. Brent Roeder discussing colorectal health, screening, and prevention. The event aims to raise awareness about rising rates of colorectal cancer in younger adults.

Sports.headline: LeBron ties Robert Parish with 1,611 NBA regular-season games
Sports.detail: LeBron James matches Robert Parish's record for NBA regular-season games played.

Sports.headline: Canada's Sarah Mitton wins shot put silver at world indoor championships
Sports.detail: Sarah Mitton secures a silver medal for Canada in shot put at the world indoor championships in Torun, Poland.

Sports.headline: 2026 NFL Free Agency Tracker: Eagles, Falcons Swap 4 Draft Picks, Safety
Sports.detail: The Eagles and Falcons made a trade involving 4 draft picks and a safety during the 2026 NFL free agency period.

Sports.headline: USA's Anthony wins 60m world gold after his blood clot 'the size of a soccer ball'
Sports.detail: Despite a prior health scare, the athlete won the 60m world gold.

Sports.headline: Elijah Wilkinson calls Falcons a 'clown show,' tells fans 'y'all in hell' after signing with Cardinals
Sports.detail: After signing with the Cardinals, Elijah Wilkinson criticizes the Falcons organization.

Entertainment.headline: Chuck Norris Dies: 'Walker, Texas Ranger' Actor Dead at 86
Entertainment.detail: Action star Chuck Norris has passed away at the age of 86, his family announced on Friday. He was best known for his role in 'Walker, Texas Ranger'.

Entertainment.headline: ABC Cancels 'Bachelorette' Season 22
Entertainment.detail: ABC has cancelled the already filmed season of 'The Bachelorette' starring Taylor Frankie Paul after a video surfaced of a 2023 incident where she was charged with assault. The reality star also temporarily lost custody of her son after a brutal fight video.

Entertainment.headline: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' box office collection day 2: Ranveer Singh's spy thriller inches close to Rs 230 crore mark
Entertainment.detail: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is nearing the Rs 230 crore mark in India after its second day in theaters. Despite a slight dip on day two, the film has made a massive impact.

Entertainment.headline: CBS News Radio to shut down after nearly a century of broadcasting
Entertainment.detail: CBS News announced Friday that CBS News Radio will be shutting down this spring after nearly 100 years of broadcasting, citing 'challenging economic realities'. The radio station is closing after nearly a century on air.

Entertainment.headline: BTS drops new studio album 'Arirang'
Entertainment.detail: K-pop super-group BTS has released their new album 'Arirang,' marking their first single after a four-year hiatus and kicking off their comeback. The album uses a 130-year-old Korean folk song and features a Hollywood star.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cop's skull smashed in creek fight as repeat offender suspect walks on $100 bond
Crime & Justice.detail: A Colorado police officer suffered a serious head injury in a violent struggle with an accused drug dealer, but the suspect was later released on a bond of just $100.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man accused of shooting father in face with crossbow captured after snowy manhunt
Crime & Justice.detail: A West Virginia man was arrested after allegedly shooting his father in the face with a crossbow and leading police on a snowy multicounty manhunt.

Crime & Justice.headline: Joseph Duggar faces child sex abuse charges
Crime & Justice.detail: Joseph Duggar, one of the stars of the reality show "19 Kids and Counting," has been arrested and is facing child sex abuse charges for allegedly abusing a 9-year-old girl six years ago in Florida.

Crime & Justice.headline: In rare move, ICE drags criminal defendant out of a federal courtroom
Crime & Justice.detail: Plainclothes agents believed to be ICE pulled a man out of a downtown federal courtroom after a pre-trial hearing, surprising the judge and defense attorneys, and legal experts say it could discourage witnesses and defendants from participating in court.

Crime & Justice.headline: 2 men suspected in Calgary murder arrested in B.C., another individual wanted
Crime & Justice.detail: Two men who were released from custody last December have been arrested again in British Columbia in connection with a murder on Dec. 10; the men face charges in connection with the fatal shooting of 32-year-old Feysal Osman Abul-Aziz, who investigators claim had connections to organized crime.

Education.headline: Saskatchewan parents face challenges of child-care costs
Education.detail: The province renegotiated its child-care deal with the feds; the old agreement did not include coverage for kids who turn six while in kindergarten.

Education.headline: Trump administration sues Harvard, alleging it failed to protect Jewish students
Education.detail: The Justice Department lawsuit filed Friday is the second time the administration has sued Harvard this year.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education Concludes Jefferson County Public Schools Violated Title IX
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education’s Office for Civil Rights concluded that Jefferson County Public Schools in Colorado has violated Title IX by permitting male students to access female bathrooms, locker rooms, and to compete in female sports.

Education.headline: Another D.C. charter school is closing as competition for students grows
Education.detail: Another D.C. charter school is closing as competition for students grows.

Education.headline: Florida professors quietly defy restrictions on race and gender: 'This is how authoritarianism works'
Education.detail: Florida professors are finding ways to work around restrictions concerning race and gender.

Lifestyle.headline: Restaurant robot goes haywire, sends tableware flying before breaking out in dance moves
Lifestyle.detail: A restaurant robot malfunctioned and sent tableware flying before unexpectedly performing dance moves.

Lifestyle.headline: Homeowners to Spend $522B on renovations in 2026 amid tight housing market
Lifestyle.detail: Despite a tight housing market, homeowners are projected to spend $522 billion on renovations in 2026.

Lifestyle.headline: Stephanie Alexander: 'I get enraged at the idea that to be healthy has become a trend' now.
Lifestyle.detail: Stephanie Alexander expresses her frustration with health becoming a trend.

Lifestyle.headline: 'It all feels very natural': Britain's sauna boom heats up as people seek warmth of human connection. now.
Lifestyle.detail: Britain is experiencing a sauna boom as people seek the warmth of human connection.

Lifestyle.headline: How Julia Child Turned Plain Ice Cream Into The Ultimate Grown-Up Dessert
Lifestyle.detail: Julia Child's recipe transforms ordinary ice cream into a sophisticated dessert.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The 'smoke-free' Taipei City vision
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taipei Mayor Chiang Wan-an announced an initiative to limit smoking in public spaces, only allowing it in designated areas, aiming for a 'smoke-free city'. The plan hopes to implement the policy to reduce smoking in public.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Taiwan-France tech pursuit
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The global technological order is rapidly restructuring, and the world is entering a new era of technological geopolitics, causing a shift in the core logic of globalization from efficiency and cost to technological competition.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Lee's unfinished 'Taiwanization'
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The political order of former president Lee Teng-hui first took shape in 1988 when he succeeded former president Chiang Ching-kuo.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on meningitis in Kent: we must not take public health systems for granted
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial expresses the importance of maintaining public health systems in light of a meningitis outbreak in Kent.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on anonymity in art: the 'unmasking' of Banksy and Ferrante should stop
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial argues against revealing the identities of anonymous artists like Banksy and Ferrante.

World.headline: Israeli military says it is striking Hezbollah targets in Beirut
World.detail: The Israeli military said early on Saturday it was striking Hezbollah targets in Beirut, after issuing an evacuation warning for seven neighborhoods in the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, with no immediate reports of casualties. Israel has been stepping up airstrikes against Lebanon in the third week of its war with Iran-backed Hezbollah.

World.headline: Iran warns world tourism sites 'not safe' for Israeli, U.S. officials
World.detail: Iran threatened to target recreational and tourist sites worldwide and insisted it was still building missiles. Iran warns that global tourist sites are unsafe for Israeli and U.S. officials, while continuing missile development amid ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Trump says considering 'winding down' Iran war but rules out ceasefire
World.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Friday that he was considering “winding down” military operations against Iran, as the United States temporarily eased sanctions on Iranian oil shipments to stem a global supply crisis. However, Trump has stated he doesn't want to do a ceasefire as the Iran war rages on.

World.headline: Kim Jong-un, daughter try out new tank
World.detail: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un and daughter Kim Ju-ae tried out the country's new battle tank in large-scale military drills, state media reported yesterday, in the latest sign that she was being groomed as his heir. The tank possesses advanced mobility, firepower and defensive systems, including protection against missiles and drones.

World.headline: The UK to allow the US to use its airbases to target Iran
World.detail: The British government has given authorisation for the United States to use military bases in Britain to carry out strikes on Iranian missile sites that are attacking ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Prime Minister Keir Starmer initially rejected a US request to use the bases.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZHRC urges tolerance on Constitutional Amendment Bill
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission (ZHRC) has called for tolerance and active public participation in ongoing hearings on the Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3, urging a respectful and robust national debate. The commission emphasized that both supporters and opponents have equal rights to be heard and encouraged citizens to participate in Parliament's public hearings.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt prioritises women's health in public institutions
Zimbabwe.detail: The Government of Zimbabwe is moving to institutionalise wellness across the civil service, highlighting that gender parity cannot be achieved while female employees face burnout and neglected health. The government is also prioritizing women's health in public institutions and tackling gender-based violence, child marriages and drug abuse, describing them as key barriers to women’s economic participation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt warns on substandard, counterfeit goods
Zimbabwe.detail: The government has issued a warning regarding substandard and counterfeit goods. Buy Zimbabwe said its collaboration with TRS was designed to help legitimate brands to safeguard their products from counterfeits.

Zimbabwe.headline: Follow the law, embassy tells Chinese firms
Zimbabwe.detail: The Chinese embassy is urging Chinese firms to follow the law. Zimbabwe’s foreign policy prioritizes trade promotion, with diplomats positioned as drivers of economic transformation.

Zimbabwe.headline: Ex-detective Muvevi denies 4 murder charges, claims goblin influence
Zimbabwe.detail: An ex-detective, Muvevi, has denied four murder charges, claiming to be under the influence of goblins. Muvevi appeared before High Court judge Justice Gibson Mandaza.
